page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and custom ARMv9-based AWS Graviton processors. It features 8 dedicated physical cores (8 vCPUs) running at 2.8 GHz, with 1 thread per core. The processor includes 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 25 Gbps.

Benchmark results show strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1897.0 and a Passmark memory score of 2866.41, both placing in the top 25%. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression reached 14.47 GB/sec, and text processing scored 2335.0, also in the top tier. Multi-core performance is average, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 13646.63 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 10093.0. In memory bandwidth tests, the instance achieved 925.02 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 154.1 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

Tradeoffs include weak performance in encryption tasks, with the Passmark CPU encryption test scoring 1510.85, which falls in the bottom 25%. Large-model LLM inference is unsupported, and medium-model prompt processing is poor at 15.47 tokens/sec. However, the instance performs well in static web serving at 20.3 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 18.36 million operations/sec. The qualitative cost efficiency of the ARM64 architecture makes this instance suitable for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, text processing, and web serving, provided local SSD storage is not required.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton processor running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 16.0 GB of RAM, 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 1897.0 and high memory bandwidth. However, the instance exhibits weak performance in CPU encryption tests and medium-model LLM prompt processing. Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated core allocation, the c8gn.2xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for scale-out compute workloads. It is highly suited for software compilation, text processing, static web serving, and Redis database operations, though workloads requiring local NVMe storage or heavy cryptographic processing may face limitations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 8 dedicated Graviton cores, 16 GB RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong single-core performance for web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 8 Graviton cores, 16 GB RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Graviton cores and high-speed networking for efficient web serving and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated ARM64 Graviton physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory with a 2.0 GB per core ratio
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
Strong single-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark results
Weak performance in cryptographic and medium-model LLM prompt processing
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and Redis databases
